title: Frontend Engineer (React)
description: Focuses on React components, lifecycle, hooks, state management, and performance.

questions.text: Explain how React handles event delegation under the hood.
How do you implement code-splitting in React using dynamic imports and React.lazy?
Explain the difference between functional and class components in React. Why did React introduce Hooks?
Explain the React reconciliation process and the role of the "key" prop in lists.
How does React Virtual DOM work under the hood, and what are the main benefits?

title: Frontend Proctored (React)
description: Live video proctored interview assessing advanced React architecture, state management, and performance.

questions.text: How do you structure a large scale React application for maintainability?
Can you walk me through how you would implement server-side rendering in a React application?
How do you handle routing and code splitting in a complex React SPA?
How do you optimize state transitions in React to prevent unnecessary child component re-renders?
Explain the concept of compound components in React and give a production example.

title: Backend Engineer (Node/Express)
description: Covers event loop, asynchronous design patterns, RESTful design, and security.

questions.text: Describe your strategy for centralized error handling and logging in a microservices architecture.
What are the pros and cons of using GraphQL over REST for a Node.js backend?
Explain how middleware execution order and flow control works in Express.js.
How does JWT authentication work, and how do you handle token revocation?
How would you design a rate-limiting middleware for an Express API?

title: HR & Behavioral Round
description: General HR behavioral questions assessing cultural fit, conflict resolution, and leadership.

questions.text: How do you proactively stay updated with the latest technological developments and frameworks?
Describe your process for prioritizing tasks when everything seems urgent and high priority.
Describe a situation where you had to quickly learn a new technology or concept.
How do you manage stress or prevent burnout during highly demanding release cycles?
Describe a time when you had to explain a complex technical concept to a non-technical stakeholder.

title: System Architect
description: Advanced systems architecture, scaling databases, caching strategy, microservices, and high-availability designs.

questions.text: Explain the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases. When do you use each architecture?
How would you architect a real-time collaborative document editor like Google Docs?
Design a ride-sharing service like Uber. Focus on the dispatcher service and geospatial querying.
How do you handle API versioning at scale without breaking client implementations?
How do you design a system for high availability and disaster recovery across multiple regions?

title: HR Interview
description: General behavioral and cultural fit questions for any role.

questions.text: What motivates you most in a professional workspace environment?
Tell me about a time you handled a difficult situation with a coworker.
What is your greatest strength and your greatest weakness?
Describe a time you showed leadership.
How do you manage professional pressure and tight production deadlines?

title: Java
description: Technical questions covering core Java, JVM, multithreading, and OOP.

questions.text: What is the difference between an abstract class and an interface?
How does garbage collection work in Java?
Explain the difference between fail-fast and fail-safe iterators in collections.
What is the Java Reflection API and in what scenarios would you use it?
Explain the difference between final, finally, and finalize keywords in Java.

title: C Programming
description: Core concepts in C including pointers, memory management, and data structures.

questions.text: Explain the difference between malloc() and calloc().
Explain the difference between stack and heap memory allocation in C.
What is a pointer to a pointer in C?
What is the purpose of the volatile keyword?
Explain the differences between a struct and a union in C.

title: Python
description: Python fundamentals, data structures, decorators, and memory management.

questions.text: How is memory managed in Python?
Explain list comprehensions with an example.
How do decorators work in Python?
How do generators work in Python and why are they memory efficient?
What is the Global Interpreter Lock (GIL)?

title: HTML
description: HTML5 features, semantics, accessibility, and DOM structure.

questions.text: How do you use meta tags for SEO and responsive display viewport configuration?
What is the purpose of the data-* attribute?
What is semantic HTML and how does it help accessibility?
What is the difference between HTML and XHTML?
How do you ensure a webpage is accessible for screen readers?

title: CSS
description: CSS Box Model, layout models (Flexbox/Grid), animations, and responsive design.

questions.text: What is the difference between Flexbox and CSS Grid?
Describe approaches to making a website responsive without media queries.
Explain the CSS Box Model.
Explain the difference between absolute, relative, fixed, and sticky positioning.
What are pseudo-classes and pseudo-elements?

title: JavaScript
description: Core JS concepts: closures, promises, event loop, and ES6+ features.

questions.text: Explain the difference between let, const, and var.
How do Promises work and how do they compare to Async/Await?
What is event bubbling and how can you stop it?
Explain the difference between call, apply, and bind.
How does the event loop handle asynchronous operations?

title: Web Development
description: General web architecture, REST APIs, HTTP protocols, and security.

questions.text: Explain the difference between HTTP and HTTPS.
How does client-side caching work using ETag and Cache-Control headers?
What is a RESTful API and what are its core principles?
What is DOM and how does browser render HTML pages?
What is DNS and how does it resolve domain names to IP addresses?

title: Authentication
description: OAuth, JWTs, session management, and securing web applications.

questions.text: What is single sign-on (SSO) and how does it work?
How do you securely store passwords in a database?
Explain the difference between OAuth2 and SAML.
What is CSRF token validation and why is it required for secure forms?
Describe the OAuth 2.0 flow.
